title: Inventory Management Assistant
description:
We are seeking a detail-oriented Inventory Management Assistant to oversee our end-to-end supply chain operations. You will be the critical link between our design studio and manufacturing partners, ensuring seamless inventory flow from factory floor to customer doorstep.



Key Responsibilities

Inventory Oversight & Reporting

Generate and analyze regular inventory reports to track stock levels, turnover rates, and demand forecasting

Monitor SKU performance and flag slow-moving or at-risk inventory

Maintain real-time visibility across multiple warehouses and fulfillment centers

Factory & Supplier Relations

Serve as primary liaison with overseas manufacturing partners (primarily Chinese-speaking factories)

Communicate production timelines, capacity constraints, and quality standards

Conduct negotiations on pricing, minimum order quantities, and lead times to optimize costs

Procurement & Purchase Orders

Prepare and issue Purchase Orders based on inventory forecasts and sales velocity

Route all POs for internal approval and payment processing

Track order status from placement through delivery, resolving delays proactively

Cross-Functional Collaboration

Partner with design team to align new product launches with inventory planning

Coordinate with logistics on inbound freight and customs clearance

Support finance team with vendor invoice reconciliation



Qualifications

2+ years experience in inventory management, supply chain, or procurement—preferably in furniture, home goods, or consumer products

Business-proficient Mandarin Chinese required for factory negotiations and relationship management

Strong proficiency in Excel/Google Sheets and inventory management software

Excellent negotiation skills with a track record of cost savings

Highly organized with ability to manage multiple SKUs and vendor relationships simultaneously

Experience with international shipping, Incoterms, and import logistics preferred

title: Executive Assistant
description:
Our founder is seeking an exceptional Executive Personal Assistant who operates as a true extension of their mind—someone who can see around corners, anticipate needs before they arise, and execute with complete autonomy. This is not a traditional EA role. You will be entrusted with high-stakes decisions across both business and personal domains, acting as the gatekeeper and enabler of the founder's vision.



We are not looking for newbies. Only candidates with 3+ years of relevant experience will be considered.



Core Mandate: Be the Eyes in the Back of Their Head

You will own the invisible infrastructure that keeps the founder operating at peak performance—professionally and personally—without requiring constant direction.



Key Responsibilities

Business Operations & Decision Proxy

Monitor all critical business channels 
, Slack, WhatsApp) and triage with surgical precision—responding on the founder's behalf where authority is delegated

Attend meetings, gather intel, and report back with synthesized insights and recommended actions

Draft communications, negotiate with vendors, and make judgment calls up to a defined threshold

Track open threads across inventory, design, finance, and partnerships; ensure nothing falls through cracks



Personal Life Architecture

Manage complex personal calendar, travel, and household operations with discretion

Handle sensitive personal matters—finances, family logistics, health appointments—treating them with the same rigor as business priorities

Build systems (not just checklists) that prevent problems before they occur



Information & Relationship Management

Be the institutional memory: track commitments, preferences, and ongoing conversations across dozens of relationships

Prepare founder for every interaction with concise, contextual briefings

Filter inbound opportunities, requests, and introductions; only surface what merits attention

Special Projects

Lead ad-hoc initiatives spanning business development, operational improvements, or personal ventures

Research, scope, and execute with minimal oversight—presenting solutions, not problems



Who You Are

Cognitive Profile

Exceptionally high IQ with rapid pattern recognition and systems thinking

Mental juggling ability: you hold 20+ threads simultaneously without dropping one

Autonomous decision-maker: you know when to act, when to ask, and when to escalate

Operational Excellence

Relentlessly organized with a bias toward action and closure

Master of invisible work: everything looks effortless because you've handled the complexity

Discreet to the core; you understand that trust is the currency of this role



Experience Requirements (Non-Negotiable)

Minimum 3 years supporting C-suite executives, founders, or high-net-worth principals—preferably in high-growth environments

Proven track record of being entrusted with significant authority and sensitive information

Experience in design, retail, e-commerce, or creative industries preferred

No exceptions on experience. We need someone who has already operated at this level, not someone learning on the job.



What Success Looks Like

Founder never asks "what's the status?" because you've already briefed them

Inbox zero achieved daily without the founder touching

Decisions made on their behalf are indistinguishable from their own choices

You anticipate needs 48-72 hours before they surface

title: Social Media Marketing Specialist (Remote)
description:
Position Overview:



We are seeking a creative and strategic Social Media Marketing Specialist to join our admissions team remotely from the Philippines. In this role, you will provide remote support to our Canadian headquarters in strengthening our online presence and expanding our international outreach—focusing on brand storytelling and community engagement. The ideal candidate will be a hands-on social media professional capable of managing our digital presence while building meaningful partnerships in the Philippines and other strategic international markets.

                                                                     



What You'll Do:



•Develop and implement an integrated social media and content strategy to boost brand visibility, audience engagement, and admissions outreach.

•Manage day-to-day social media operations—including content creation, scheduling, publishing, and community engagement—across Instagram,
, TikTok, and other relevant platforms.

•Produce compelling multimedia content such as stories, newsletters, photos, and videos that capture authentic school life, student achievements, and campus culture.

•Proactively identify and build relationships with educational institutions and partners in the Philippines as well as other strategic international markets—to explore collaborations and expand the school’s global network.

•Monitor, analyze, and report on social media and partnership performance to refine strategy and drive continuous improvement.



What We're Looking For:



•Bachelor's degree in Communications, Marketing, Digital Media, or related field.



•3+ years of hands-on experience managing social media channels for market expansion.



•Proven experiences in building relationships with agencies or partners (Education sector is a plus).



•Excellent English storytelling, writing, and communication skills



•A good desktop computer or a laptop with reliable internet connection



•Highly organized, proactive individual capable of managing multiple projects, with a strong interest in international education and cross-cultural partnership development; 



To apply, please include the following with your application:

•Your Resume

•Link to your portfolio





About Us:



Founded in 2013, Canada Star Education Group (
) is an international education company headquartered in vibrant Vancouver, British Columbia. Registered and certified by the British Columbia Ministry of Education, the group comprises three distinct educational divisions: Canada Star Secondary School (an elite private school for grades 10–12), Windsor Hall (an IB World School for grades 6–9) and Can-Star Academy (after-school tutoring programs). Guided by our motto “Confidence, Perseverance, Passion, Inclusion,” we are committed to nurturing students' holistic development and preparing them to become tomorrow’s global leaders.

title: Customer Support (Written) | No Experience Needed | FUN| Dance Academy
description:
***READ FULL JOB POST BEFORE APPLYING. *** Please don't waste your time or ours. Thanks :)



Full-time, long-term opportunity.



Four days per week, or five days per week scheduling options. 



Looking for:

- Outstanding English

- Empathy



Our communications with students are FRIENDLY, FUN, and PERSONAL.



ABOUT US

We are the largest salsa & bachata dance academy in Los Angeles and are growing rapidly.



The culture here is FUN, fast, and friendly. Our goal is to make every student feel welcome and completely taken care of.



Dancing changes lives - we are on a mission to deliver the best salsa/bachata educational experience on Earth.



The company is MaS Dance Academy.





ABOUT YOUR TEAM

Working with a remote team that helps with all facets of the business, you'll form a close working relationship and bond with your team mates. We even host team retreats in the Philippines!



----------------------------

SCHEDULE

All times written in PST (Los Angeles time)



TRAINING (2-4 weeks):

Sunday - Thursday 9am - 6pm (with 1 hour break)



AFTER TRAINING



Both options are full-time 40 hours per week. 



OPTION 1 - FIVE DAY SCHEDULE:

Thursday - Monday 8am - 5pm (with 1 hour break)



OPTION 2 - FOUR DAY SCHEDULE:

Saturday, Tuesday, Wednesday 10am - 9pm (with 1 hour break)

Friday 12pm (noon) - 11pm (with 1 hour break)

----------------------------



YOUR ROLE

• Your primary role is written student support (text,
 etc.)

• When there are no support tasks to complete, you'll contribute in a wide variety of other ways, depending on your skills and interests. Some examples may include writing blogs, updating websites, doing research, collecting and analyzing numerical data and using it to create reports, etc.





WRITTEN VS PHONE SUPPORT

• 95% of our student support is written. However, occasionally you will need to be on the phone with a student. You must be comfortable on the phone.





TYPES OF SUPPORT YOU'LL HANDLE

• One on one texting and
ing

• Mass texting and
ing

• Occasional phone calls (rare)

• Updating database after every interaction and attempted interaction

• Daily reports

• Etc.





COMPANY CULTURE

Working in a small business like ours is very different from a large corporation. Some of the differences are:



• Personal tone: Our communications with students are FRIENDLY, FUN, and PERSONAL. It's like talking to your best friend. We use emoji's and exclamation points and are very personal. We take the opposite approach of large corporations that give robotic, frustrating, not very helpful support. You need to be intuitive and have strong emotional intelligence to feel what the students are needing when they communicate.



• More creativity: there is more freedom to adjust your responses for each scenario. This is more challenging than just following strict templates, but also more stimulating and exciting.



• Impact: your actions will have a big impact on the business, which you will clearly see



• Feedback: your feedback on what we are doing well, and what could be better, is not only valued, but is mandatory! We want everyone here thinking as if they were the business owner.





ABOUT YOU

• Perfect written English

• Available full-time

• High emotional intelligence

• Flexible

• Quick learner

• Thrives in fast-paced, quickly changing environment

• Desire to always seek better ways to do things, including finding ways to use A.I. tools

• Happy to be on the phone when needed (which is rarely)

• Tremendous attention to detail - gets things right the first time

• PRO-ACTIVE. Always looking for ways to improve.

• Meticulous, organized, methodical. Almost never misses a detail.

• Critical, creative thinker. Not robotic. Always looking for better ways to do things. Always considering all the angles of a task.

• Not afraid to share your opinion - we NEED to hear your opinion on the approaches that we take so that we can improve.

• Able to ask clarifying questions in order to get tasks right the first time

• Strong communicator

• Makes decisions independently

• Quick learner

• Fast computer

• Fast, dependable Internet

• Bonus if you have experience or interest in salsa or bachata or any dance!





APPLY NOW. FORMAT YOUR APPLICATION AS A NUMBERED LIST WITH THE FOLLOWING:

1. A brief intro about yourself



2. Are you happy to be on the phone when needed?



3. This is a fast-paced environment with quickly changing conditions. You’ll need to be able to think quickly on your feet and track dozens of moving pieces at once with precision and accuracy. This role is NOT for people who feel overly anxious in this type of environment. Apply only if you thrive in such an environment. Based on this information, do you still feel this role is a good fit for you?



4. Complete this form: 
/
. Write in the subject line of your application the answers to the assessment in the link above: 1. Grammar score 2. Typing WPM 3. Numbers assessment answer (regarding achieving $2,000 profit) 4. Highest level of formal education



6. Any customer support experience? If yes, please describe.



7. Which of the two scheduling options do you prefer?



8. These positions are competitive, often with hundreds of applicants. What is your competitive requested monthly payment for this full-time position?





(Don't
 us off the platform or message on social media - your application will be disqualified. Don't worry, we personally check every application here)



Looking forward to meeting you!

title: Airbnb Claims Manager
description:
Role Overview

We are hiring an experienced Airbnb Claims Manager to handle all post checkout claims across our portfolio. You must have previous experience working in Airbnb Support and understand how to navigate claims, documentation requirements and review removal processes from the inside. Your role will focus on filing accurate claims, progressing them with support, and actioning review removals where eligible. The hours will be Monday - Saturday, 11am - 6pm.



Key Responsibilities

1. File all Airbnb Resolution Centre claims for damages, missing items, excess cleaning and breaches

2. Work directly with Airbnb Support using your platform experience to move claims to resolution

3. Prepare and upload evidence including photos, invoices, reports and message logs

4. Track all claims, maintain organised documentation and update internal records

5. Identify and action reviews eligible for removal based on Airbnb’s policy

6. Communicate professionally with guests when follow up is required

7. Flag any high risk cases or escalations to the management team

8. Coordinate with operations to quickly gather required evidence or details

9. Responds to all support cases throughout all platforms during your shift and dispute refunds if necessary



Requirements

1. Must have previously worked for Airbnb Support handling claims or trust and safety matters

2. Strong written English with clear and confident communication

3. High attention to detail and an organised approach to evidence and documentation

4. Ability to work independently and follow structured processes

5. Reliable internet and ability to work Australian hours

title: Full Stack Developer (CRO & E-Commerce Specialist)
description:
Role Overview:



You’ll be the technical backbone for our Shopify and Next Commerce stores, implementing features that are often first prototyped via Vibe code. You will work closely with our team to implement CRO strategies, optimize landing pages, tweak upsells, and manage ad-driven content experiments.



This role is not just about coding—it’s about understanding how to turn traffic into revenue. You will need to be proactive, self-learning, and able to execute independently while collaborating with marketing and content teams.



Key Responsibilities:



Implement, customize, and maintain Shopify and Next Commerce stores.



Convert vibe-coded features into fully functional, production-ready implementations.



Build, test, and optimize landing pages, upsells, and offers with a focus on conversion.



Work closely with marketing teams to implement ad-driven content and CRO experiments.



Troubleshoot and debug front-end and back-end issues efficiently.



Maintain clean, organized, and scalable code.



Collaborate with designers to ensure designs are technically feasible and optimized for performance.



Contribute ideas for improving UX, CRO, and overall store performance.



Requirements:



Proven experience as a full stack developer (Shopify and Next Commerce preferred).



Solid understanding of front-end technologies (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) and back-end systems.



CRO-focused mindset; experience implementing A/B tests, landing page optimization, and upsells.



Familiarity with vibe-coded features is a plus.



Bonus: Design skills to help translate creative ideas into functional pages.



Ability to work independently and proactively troubleshoot issues.



Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.



Excellent communication skills to work across teams and provide updates.



Preferred Experience:



Previous experience in an e-commerce agency or ads-driven environment.



Understanding of ad funnels, ad-driven content, and dynamic content integration.



Self-learning and proactive experimentation mindset.



How to Apply:



Please submit your resume along with examples of Shopify/Next Commerce projects you’ve implemented, especially highlighting any CRO improvements or landing page optimizations.

title: High Conversion Video Editor
description:
Role Overview



As our Video Editor, you’ll be responsible for producing conversion-focused videos for social media, ads, product launches, and website content. You’ll bring our brand story to life visually while maintaining a strong understanding of what drives viewers to act.



Key Responsibilities



Edit and produce high-quality, conversion-focused videos for social media platforms (TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts,
, etc.).



Craft video content optimized for eCommerce campaigns, including product demos, tutorials, testimonials, and UGC-style content.



Collaborate with the marketing team to understand campaign objectives and translate them into compelling video assets.



Incorporate motion graphics, animations, and effects to enhance storytelling and engagement.



Maintain brand consistency across all video content, ensuring tone, style, and messaging align with the brand’s voice.



Stay up-to-date on video trends, platform requirements, and best practices for conversion and engagement.



Review performance data and adjust video content to improve click-through and conversion rates.



Qualifications



Proven experience editing videos for eCommerce, preferably skincare, beauty, or wellness brands.



Strong portfolio of short-form videos that have driven measurable conversions or engagement.



Proficiency in video editing software (Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, After Effects, or similar).



Understanding of social media platforms’ video requirements and best practices.



Ability to create fast-paced, eye-catching edits that hook viewers in the first 3 seconds.



Creative storytelling skills and attention to detail.



Strong organizational skills and ability to meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.



Bonus: Experience with A/B testing video creatives and analyzing performance metrics.



What We Offer



Opportunity to work with a growing skincare brand with an engaged audience.



Remote flexibility and creative freedom to experiment with video concepts.



Competitive compensation based on experience and results.



A collaborative team passionate about beauty, health, and digital marketing.



How to Apply



Send your resume, portfolio (must include ecommerce/skincare content), and a brief note on why you’re the perfect fit for this role. Include examples of videos that drove engagement or conversions.

title: Millwork Drafter (AutoCAD)
description:
Millwork company is seeking an experienced Millwork Drafter to produce precise, fabrication-ready shop drawings for high-end custom architectural millwork projects.

This is a production drafting position. The role focuses exclusively on AutoCAD shop drawings used directly for manufacturing and installation - not design development, 3D visualization, or rendering.

We are looking for a drafter who understands millwork construction, ideally with experience gained through prior drafting work in a millwork shop or hands-on experience in cabinet fabrication and installation. The ideal candidate understands how approved shop drawings translate into cutting, machining, assembly, and field installation.



Responsibilities

- Produce detailed, production-ready millwork shop drawings in AutoCAD based on architectural drawings and project scope

- Develop complete drawing sets including plans, elevations, sections, and fabrication details

- Fully dimension all components, materials, finishes, hardware, joinery, and clearances

- Integrate appliance specifications and specialty hardware requirements directly into drawings

- Apply professional millwork construction standards including seam layout, grain direction, tolerances, reveals, and installation clearances

- Revise drawings based on internal redlines and coordination feedback

- Maintain disciplined file organization, naming conventions, and version control

- Prepare coordinated PDF and CAD packages for internal review and shop release

- Communicate technical questions and drawing status clearly with Project Managers



Requirements

- Strong proficiency in AutoCAD (required)

- Minimum 3 years of experience producing millwork shop drawings for fabrication

- Direct experience working in a millwork or cabinet manufacturing environment

- Solid understanding of cabinet construction, joinery methods, hardware integration, and installation sequencing

- Ability to interpret architectural drawings and specifications

- High level of accuracy, technical discipline, and attention to detail

- Conversational English for coordination with PMs and internal teams



Not a Fit If

- Your background is primarily in 3D modeling, rendering, or interior design visualization

- You do not have real production-level millwork experience

- You have not prepared shop drawings that were used for actual fabrication



Nice to Have

- Hands-on cabinet assembly or installation experience

- Experience coordinating appliance integration and specialty hardware

- Familiarity with US architectural standards and field coordination



What We Offer

- Steady pipeline of high-end custom millwork projects

- Clear drafting standards and structured workflows

- Direct collaboration with experienced project and production teams

- Long-term opportunity with a growing millwork company



Application Instructions

Send
 samples of your most recent production-level millwork shop drawings to:



In the
 subject line, enter:

Drafting Portfolio + Your First and Last Name

Ensure all attachments are accessible and clearly labeled.

ABOUT US







Young Publisher is an international digital marketing company working with content creators and online brands.



BENEFITS



- Full-time position: 8 hours daily, Monday-Friday; 40 hours a week

- Knowledge of Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts

WHY JOIN US?



- Start rate: $935 a month + performance-based bonuses (TOTAL: $1,100 a month)

Note: More experienced CSMs earn between $1,800 and $2,500 after 6-12 months.

- Performance bonuses: Up to $900+ additional monthly based on:

- Client retention rates

- Task completion metrics

- Quality of feedback

- Team collaboration

- Payments: Twice per month via Wise directly to your bank

- Tools access: We'll cover all the needed tools

- Work-life balance: No graveyard shifts

- Professional growth: Regular feedback and skill development

#1: Your CV.

title: FULL-TIME VIRTUAL ASSISTANT — US Environmental Consulting Firm (DFW, Texas)
description:
FULL-TIME VIRTUAL ASSISTANT — US Environmental Consulting Firm (DFW, Texas)



Vertexium Environmental Solutions LLC | Remote (Philippines) | Full-Time, 40 hrs/week | CST Hours



================================================================================



ABOUT THE COMPANY



Vertexium Environmental Solutions LLC is a US-based environmental consulting firm serving the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex and North Texas. We specialize in Phase I and Phase II Environmental Site Assessments, environmental due diligence, and remediation management for commercial real estate, industrial, oil and gas, construction, and healthcare clients.



Our firm is led by Dr. Mohamed Elansary (PhD, Environmental Engineering), who provides senior-level technical oversight on every project. We work with commercial developers, property managers, industrial operators, construction firms, and attorneys across the DFW area.



We are hiring a Virtual Assistant to serve as our Client Relations Specialist — the frontline of our business development operation. This is not a data entry role. You will be the voice and face of a US professional services firm, engaging directly with executives, decision-makers, and business owners via phone,
, and LinkedIn.



================================================================================



THE ROLE



You will own the full top-of-funnel sales process: identifying prospects, reaching out, qualifying leads, booking discovery calls, and managing the CRM pipeline. You will represent the firm in all outbound and inbound communications with US-based clients and prospects.



This role requires someone who is sharp, self-directed, and comfortable speaking with C-suite executives and senior professionals in the United States. You must sound professional on the phone and write
s that are indistinguishable from a native English speaker.



CORE RESPONSIBILITIES:



-- Lead Generation & Research

   * Identify and qualify prospects using LinkedIn Sales Navigator

   * Research companies in commercial real estate, oil & gas, petrochemical, construction, healthcare, industrial, and manufacturing sectors

   * Build and maintain a pipeline of qualified leads in the CRM

   * Add 30+ new qualified leads to the CRM daily



-- Cold Outreach & Appointment Setting

   * Execute multi-channel outreach: cold calls,
s, and LinkedIn messages

   * Follow structured outreach sequences 
, LinkedIn, phone)

   * Send 50+ outreach touches per day across all channels

   * Book discovery calls with qualified prospects

   * Target: 5+ discovery calls booked per week



-- Discovery Calls

   * Conduct 30-minute discovery calls with prospects

   * Qualify prospects based on project type, timeline, budget, and decision-making authority

   * Present the firm's services professionally and match prospects to the right solution

   * Draft proposals and statements of work following established guidelines



-- CRM Management (GoHighLevel)

   * Manage the full sales pipeline: New Lead through Won/Lost

   * Keep all contact records updated with notes, tags, and activity logs

   * Track outreach sequences and follow-up schedules

   * Generate daily and weekly activity reports



-- Client Communication

   * Respond to all inbound inquiries within 2 hours during business hours

   * Handle follow-up calls,
s, and messages with warm leads

   * Represent the firm with professionalism and confidence in every interaction



-- Social Media (LinkedIn)

   * Send connection requests and engage with prospects on LinkedIn

   * Post periodic updates on the company LinkedIn page

   * Monitor and respond to engagement on company content



================================================================================



WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU



This is a performance-driven role with clear daily and weekly targets. You will be measured on leads generated, outreach volume, calls booked, and pipeline value — not hours logged. We want someone who takes ownership, works independently, and treats this as their career, not a side gig.



Daily targets:

   * 30 new leads added to CRM

   * 50+ outreach messages sent 
 + LinkedIn + calls)

   * All inbound inquiries responded to within 2 hours



Weekly targets:

   * 5+ discovery calls booked

   * 3-5 discovery calls completed

   * 2-3 proposals sent

   * $20K-$50K pipeline value added



You will submit a structured daily end-of-day report summarizing your activity, pipeline status, and priorities for the next day.



================================================================================



REQUIREMENTS — PLEASE READ CAREFULLY BEFORE APPLYING



If you do not meet ALL of the following requirements, please do not apply. We will not consider incomplete or generic applications.



MUST-HAVE:



1. NEUTRAL OR AMERICAN-SOUNDING ACCENT — NON-NEGOTIABLE

   You will be calling US-based executives, VPs, and business owners on the phone. Your accent must be clear, neutral, and professional. Heavy accents that make phone conversations difficult for US listeners will not work for this role. This is the single most important qualification.



2. GoHighLevel (GHL) EXPERIENCE — OR STRONG CRM EXPERIENCE

   Direct GHL experience is strongly preferred. If you have not used GHL, you must have substantial hands-on experience with HubSpot, Salesforce, Pipedrive, or a similar CRM — and be willing and able to learn GHL within your first week. You must understand pipelines, contact management, tagging, workflows, and reporting.



3. SALES & OUTREACH EXPERIENCE

   You must have proven experience in cold calling, cold
ing, appointment setting, or lead generation. We need someone who has actually picked up the phone, pitched a service, handled objections, and booked meetings. If your outreach experience is limited to sending template messages, this is not the right fit.



4. EXCELLENT WRITTEN ENGLISH

   Your
s, LinkedIn messages, and proposals must read as native-level professional English. No grammatical errors, no awkward phrasing. You will be writing to executives at US companies — the writing must be sharp.



5. RELIABLE INTERNET CONNECTION & QUIET WORKSPACE

   You will be on calls with US clients. Background noise, dropped calls, or unstable connections are unacceptable. You must have a dedicated workspace with a stable internet connection (minimum 25 Mbps) and a quality headset with noise cancellation.



6. AVAILABLE FOR CST HOURS — 8:00 AM TO 5:00 PM CENTRAL TIME

   This is non-negotiable. Our clients are in Texas. You must be available and actively working during these hours, Monday through Friday.



7. OWN LAPTOP/COMPUTER

   You must have your own reliable computer with a webcam and headset. We do not provide equipment.



NICE-TO-HAVE (NOT REQUIRED, BUT WILL SET YOU APART):



   * Experience in B2B professional services (consulting, engineering, legal, financial)

   * Familiarity with environmental consulting, real estate, or construction industries

   * Experience with LinkedIn Sales Navigator

   * Experience with PandaDoc or proposal/contract software

   * Experience with Google Workspace (Gmail, Drive, Docs, Calendar)

   * Previous work with US-based companies in a client-facing role



================================================================================



SCHEDULE & WORK ARRANGEMENT



   * Remote — work from home (Philippines-based)

   * Full-time: 40 hours per week, Monday through Friday

   * Hours: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM Central Standard Time (CST/CDT)

   * Start date: As soon as possible

   * Long-term position — this is not a short-term project. We are looking for someone who wants to grow with the company.



================================================================================



WHAT YOU GET



   * Stable, long-term full-time employment with a growing US firm

   * Direct mentorship and training from a PhD-level technical expert

   * Clear daily structure and SOPs — you will never wonder "what should I do today"

   * Performance-based growth — prove yourself and your responsibilities (and compensation) will expand

   * Professional development in the environmental consulting and commercial real estate industries

   * A role where your work directly drives company revenue — this is not busy work



================================================================================



HOW TO APPLY — READ THIS SECTION COMPLETELY



We receive many applications. To ensure we review yours, you MUST include ALL of the following. Applications missing any of these items will be automatically rejected.



1. VOICE RECORDING (REQUIRED — THIS IS YOUR ACCENT SCREENING)

   Record a 1-2 minute audio or video of yourself. In the recording:

   * Introduce yourself (name, location, years of experience)

   * Briefly describe your most relevant work experience

   * Explain why you want to work for a US environmental consulting firm

   * Speak naturally — do not read from a script. We want to hear how you actually sound on a call.

   Upload to Google Drive, Loom, Vocaroo, or any accessible link and include it in your application.



2. ANSWER THESE THREE QUESTIONS IN YOUR COVER LETTER:

   Applications that skip any question will be rejected.



   Question 1: "Describe your experience with GoHighLevel or a similar CRM. What specific features did you use, and what did you use them for? If you have not used GHL, describe the CRM you used most recently and how you managed a sales pipeline with it."



   Question 2: "Tell me about a specific time you booked a meeting or appointment through cold outreach (cold call, cold
, or LinkedIn). What was the prospect's industry and title? What was your approach, and what did you say or write that got them to agree?"



   Question 3: "Why do you want to work for a US-based environmental consulting firm? What interests you about this industry, and what makes you confident you can represent the firm professionally to US executives?"



3. YOUR RESUME/CV

   Include your updated resume with relevant work experience highlighted.



================================================================================



IMPORTANT: APPLICATIONS THAT DO NOT INCLUDE THE VOICE RECORDING AND ANSWERS TO ALL THREE QUESTIONS WILL NOT BE REVIEWED. NO EXCEPTIONS.



We are looking for one exceptional person, not a stack of generic applications. If you take the time to submit a complete, thoughtful application, you will stand out.



We look forward to hearing from you.



— Vertexium Environmental Solutions LLC

   DFW, Texas |

title: Virtual CRM & Data Specialist
description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

This organization is a mission-driven nonprofit headquartered in the United States. It operates a national

network of grassroots chapters focused on advocacy, education, and community organizing. The organization

partners with progressive groups nationwide to advance equity, inclusion, and social impact through coalition

building and grassroots engagement.



ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a detail-oriented, process-driven Virtual CRM & Data Specialist to support the Chief Operating

Officer. This is a part-time role (~20 hours/week) ideal for a professional who thrives on bringing order to data,

improving organizational systems, and maintaining the integrity of CRM and
 marketing operations.

The primary focus will be cleaning, organizing, and maintaining the organization's CRM database and

supporting
 marketing list hygiene. The right candidate will not only execute assigned tasks but will

proactively identify gaps in processes and recommend best practices to strengthen the organization's data

infrastructure.



This is a growth-oriented position with a clear path from part-time to full-time based on performance and

organizational need.



RESPONSIBILITIES



CRM Data Management (Primary Focus)

1. Conduct a comprehensive cleanup of the organization's CRM database, including deduplication,

standardization of contact records, and correction of data entry errors.

Recruited by Opus Spark | opusspark.co Page 1

2. Maintain ongoing data hygiene by regularly auditing records, merging duplicates, and ensuring all

contact information is current and accurately categorized.

3. Input new contact data into the CRM following conferences, events, campaigns, and other outreach

activities, ensuring consistent formatting and proper tagging.

4. Organize and segment contact lists to support targeted outreach, fundraising, and engagement

campaigns.



 Marketing Support

1. Clean and maintain
 marketing lists to improve deliverability rates and reduce bounce rates.

2. Identify and remove inactive, invalid, or duplicate
 addresses through regular list hygiene audits.

3. Support
 campaign operations by ensuring accurate list segmentation and contact tagging prior to

sends.

4. Monitor
 performance data and flag trends or issues related to list health for leadership review.



Reporting & Dashboard Creation

1. Build and maintain reports and dashboards within the CRM and project management platforms to give

the team clear visibility into key metrics, campaign performance, and funnel activity.

2. Identify opportunities to automate recurring reports so leadership can access up-to-date data without

manual pulls.

3. Translate raw CRM and
 data into actionable insights, surfacing trends and patterns that support

organizational decision-making.



Process Development and Improvement

1. Identify gaps in existing workflows related to CRM management, data entry, and
 marketing

operations.

2. Develop and document standard operating procedures (SOPs) and best practices for data management

and list maintenance.

3. Flag opportunities for process improvement proactively, recommending solutions to leadership before

issues escalate.

4. Maintain organized records within the project management platform to track task progress, deadlines,

and ongoing projects.



REQUIREMENTS



Must-Have Qualifications

1. 2+ years of experience working with CRM platforms, with hands-on experience in data cleanup, data

entry, and database management.

2. Experience with
 marketing tools, including list hygiene, segmentation, and deliverability

optimization.

3. Proven ability to develop and document standard operating procedures or organizational best practices.

4. Exceptional attention to detail with a commitment to data accuracy and consistency.

5. Strong data stewardship mindset. Must approach CRM records with care and caution, understanding

that organizational data is valuable and irreplaceable. Never delete, merge, or modify records without

proper verification and a clear rationale.

Recruited by Opus Spark | opusspark.co Page 2

6. Ability to work independently, manage time effectively, and deliver outputs without constant oversight.

7. Strong written communication skills for documenting processes and communicating findings to

leadership.

8. Reliable internet connection and ability to overlap communication with U.S. time zones.



Preferred Qualifications

1. Experience with nonprofit organizing CRM platforms such as EveryAction, NGP VAN, Salesforce

Nonprofit, or similar.

2. Hands-on experience with project management tools such as 
, Asana, or similar, including task tracking, workflows, automations, and team coordination.

3. Background in supporting nonprofit, advocacy, or mission-driven organizations.

4. Experience with Google Suite (Gmail, Sheets, Docs, Drive) in a professional setting.

5. Understanding of
 marketing metrics such as open rates, bounce rates, and deliverability, along

with best practices for list management.

6. Experience building reports, dashboards, or automated data views within a CRM or project management

platform.

7. Comfort with learning new tools and technologies quickly. A growth mindset is essential.



KEY TRAITS WE'RE LOOKING FOR

1. Process-Oriented: Has a natural instinct for creating structure and order. Sees gaps in workflows and

proactively builds or recommends solutions rather than waiting to be told.

2. Detail-Obsessed: Treats data accuracy as non-negotiable. Double-checks entries, catches

inconsistencies, and takes pride in maintaining a clean, reliable database.

3. Careful Data Steward: Understands that organizational data is precious and irreplaceable. Approaches

every cleanup task with caution, always verifying before deleting, merging, or modifying records. Asks

questions first, acts second.

4. Self-Starter: Works independently with minimal supervision. Takes initiative, manages their own

workload, and delivers consistent results without needing to be micromanaged.

5. Transparent Communicator: Speaks up when something is unclear, flags issues early, and

communicates progress honestly. No surprises. Leadership values knowing where things stand.

6. Mission-Aligned: Understands and respects the purpose behind the organization's work. While deep

subject matter expertise is not required, genuine respect for the organization's mission and values is

expected.

7. Team Player: Treated as an integral part of the team, not a separate contractor. Expected to contribute

ideas, flag opportunities, and grow into greater responsibility over time.



TOOLS AND PLATFORMS

Tool Category Proficiency

Nonprofit CRM Platform CRM &
 Marketing Required (will train on specifics)

Recruited by Opus Spark | opusspark.co Page 3

Project Management Tool Project Management Required (prior experience

preferred)

Google Suite
 & Collaboration Required



HOW TO APPLY

Interested candidates will complete a pre-recorded video interview and submit their resume for review. Top

candidates will be shortlisted and forwarded to the client team for final evaluation.

This is a tremendous opportunity to join a growing, mission-driven nonprofit with a clear scope of work, a

supportive leadership team, and real potential to grow into an expanded role over time.

title: TikTok Shop Admin Manager
description:
Company Overview – Pearl West



Pearl West is a leading eCommerce investment and operating group specializing in acquiring, optimizing, and scaling premium brands. As part of our growing TikTok Shop operations, we are seeking a detail-oriented and tech-savvy individual to manage all backend administrative tasks critical to maintaining high-performing TikTok Shops.



At Pearl West, We believe in building a team that takes full responsibility for outcomes, moves with urgency, and strives to outperform at every level. Our culture encourages initiative, accountability, and a commitment to high standards—empowering individuals to own their work, embrace challenges, and continuously elevate performance in a fast-moving, competitive landscape. The ideal candidate will help us execute, analyze, and refine backend systems across multiple brands while supporting shop performance and campaign success.



Role Overview

We are looking for a TikTok Shop Admin Manager to take ownership of day-to-day backend operations for multiple TikTok Shops. This role involves managing product listings, enrolling SKUs into campaigns, analyzing account performance, product pricing, ensuring compliance, and driving operational efficiency.



The ideal candidate is highly organized, proactive, and data-driven—comfortable juggling multiple platforms, deadlines, and updates in a fast-paced eCommerce environment. Experience with TikTok Shop tools and seller dashboards is a strong plus.



This is a full-time, remote position with direct involvement in marketing, design testing, reporting, and compliance management.



Key Responsibilities

Manage Product Listings

– Create, edit, and optimize product listings to ensure accuracy, compliance, discoverability, and alignment with brand standards.



Maintain Product Pricing & Profitability

– Monitor and adjust product pricing based on cost of goods, platform fees, and marketing performance. Track SKU-level profitability to ensure margin targets are met and flag underperforming listings for review.



Enroll in TikTok Campaigns

– Submit eligible SKUs into promotional campaigns, track enrollment windows, monitor discount structures, and evaluate campaign performance impact.



Monitor Shop Health & Violations

– Proactively track shop warnings, product takedowns, and policy violations. Resolve issues swiftly to maintain optimal account health and minimize disruption.



Analyze Performance Metrics

– Review key analytics including sales velocity, traffic, conversion rate, and top-performing SKUs. Identify trends, surface anomalies, and recommend optimizations.



Track & Report Daily Numbers

– Deliver daily reports summarizing performance across all active shops. Highlight wins, red flags, campaign outcomes, and emerging opportunities.



Test Product Imagery & Listings

– Collaborate with creative teams to A/B test product images and listing copy. Track impact on click-through and conversion rates to inform future iterations.



Stay Updated on Platform Features

– Keep current with TikTok Shop updates, seller tools, algorithm shifts, and feature rollouts. Evaluate and implement relevant changes to improve backend workflows.



Coordinate with Marketing & Ops

– Liaise with creators, marketers, and operations teams to ensure product listings are synchronized with inventory status, campaign schedules, and promotional goals.



Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)

Listing Accuracy: Maintain 99%+ listing compliance and error-free submissions.

Product Optimization: Optimize product listing images for the product catalogue 

Violation Response Time: Address all violations within 24 hours.

Campaign Participation: Enter 100% of eligible SKUs into relevant campaigns.

Performance Reporting: Deliver daily reports to TikTok Shop manager

Image Test Implementation: Launch and track results of 2–3 image or copy tests per week.



Success Benchmarks (
Days)

First 30 Days

? Complete onboarding, gain familiarity with TikTok Shop dashboard, and understand all current listings across brands.

? Begin submitting daily reports and assist with minor listing updates and campaign submissions.

? Audit shops for past violations or listing errors and propose a cleanup plan.



First 60 Days

? Own all product uploads, edits, and campaign enrollments across multiple brands.

? Launch and evaluate results of your first image/copy A/B tests.

? Create templates for streamlined reporting and listing audits.



First 90 Days

? Maintain violation-free shops through proactive monitoring and quick responses.

? Improve listing performance (CTR or conversion) through tested optimizations.

? Share weekly insights and improvement suggestions based on analytics



 

Required Skills & Qualifications

Must-Have Skills:



Strong attention to detail and task ownership

Experience managing eCommerce product listings and platforms (Amazon, TikTok Shop, Shopify, etc.)

Proficiency with Excel/Sheets, analytics dashboards, and reporting tools

Ability to interpret performance metrics and make optimization decisions

Excellent organizational and time management skills

Nice-to-Have Skills:



Familiarity with TikTok Shop Seller Center and campaign tools

Prior experience in backend operations, compliance, or data analysis

Experience working with cross-functional teams (marketing, creative, operations)

Understanding of performance marketing or TikTok algorithm behavior



Who Should Apply?

? Apply if you:



Thrive in fast-paced, detail-heavy roles

Love structure, checklists, and refining workflows

Have experience with digital storefronts or backend operations

Are naturally proactive, analytical, and organized



???? Do not apply if:



Prefer high-level strategy over hands-on execution

Struggle with attention to detail or managing multiple dashboards

Have limited experience with performance reporting or product listings



Why Join Pearl West?

Build & Lead a Thriving Community – Play a key role in shaping a growing creator-focused community.

Collaborate with Industry Leaders – Work alongside marketing professionals, content creators, and eCommerce experts.

Growth & Career Development – Opportunities to expand your role and impact in the digital marketing space.

Flexible Work Setup – Work remotely, hybrid, or in-office.

Competitive Compensation – Salary TBD based on experience + performance-based bonuses.



Compensation & Benefits

Salary: $2000-$2500 (based on experience)

Bonus/Commission Structure: Performance-based bonuses available

Work Arrangement: Remote

Health & Wellness Benefits: Medical, dental, vision, mental health support

Additional Perks: Flexible PTO, company retreats, learning stipends



Submit your application through this link: 



Please note that applications submitted through this channel will not be considered.

ds (a must)

title: Process Automation Specialist, Remote (PH)
description:
About RocketAMS

RocketAMS is an Australian e-commerce agency managing marketplace operations for established brands. We handle catalogue strategy, advertising, reporting, and channel expansion across Amazon and other Australian platforms. We're scaling fast and need someone to build the operational systems that make it possible.



The Role

Your job is to look at what the team does manually, and automate things

You'll map out our workflows, identify the repetitive parts, and build systems that handle them — using AI tools, workflow automation, and smart spreadsheet models. The key constraint: everything you build needs to be maintainable by non-technical tea
mbers. If something breaks, an account manager should be able to fix it, not just you.



That means we favour no-code/low-code tools over pure code. Zapier and n8n over Python scripts. Claude and ChatGPT interfaces over custom API builds. Google Sheets over databases. When code is genuinely needed, it should be simple, documented, and the last resort.



What You'll Do

Audit existing team workflows and identify what can be automated, systematised, or eliminated

Build automations using Zapier, n8n, or similar workflow tools — connecting our platforms, triggering actions, moving data between systems without manual intervention

Use AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT) extensively — both through the frontend interfaces and via API integrations where needed — to automate content generation, data processing, analysis, and reporting tasks

Build configurable Excel/Sheets models — scoring engines, decision frameworks, dashboards, templates — where every input is a variable and nothing is hardcoded

Take raw data from client systems (ERPs, ad platforms, e-commerce channels) and clean, structure, and transform it into something the team can act on

Design standardised reporting templates and automated pipelines across all clients

Write clear SOPs and documentation so anyone on the team can use, update, and troubleshoot your tools without technical knowledge

Continuously look for new AI tools, automations, and workflow improvements — this space moves fast and we want to stay ahead



Requirements:

Hands-on experience with workflow automation tools — Zapier, n8n, Make, or similar

Heavy user of AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT) — both frontend and API. You use these daily and understand how to build prompts, workflows, and integrations around them

Advanced Excel / Google Sheets — INDEX/MATCH, nested IFs, dynamic ranges, conditional formatting. You've built complex multi-tab models that don't break when inputs change

Systems thinking — you see how inputs connect to outputs and build things that are configurable, not static

Bias toward simple, maintainable solutions — you'd rather build something a non-technical person can update than something clever that only you understand

Ability to clean messy, unstructured data and make it usable

Strong attention to detail

Fluent written English — you'll write SOPs, process docs, and methodology notes

Available for at least 4 hours daily overlap with Australian Eastern Time



Benefits:

Paid Time Off

Work From Home

Training & Development

Private Health Insurance After 12 Months

Day Shift - 7am to 4pm PH Time



Why Join Us?

Work with ex-Amazonians and industry experts.

Fast-growing agency with opportunities to lead and grow.

Flexible, remote-friendly work environment.



We are accepting applications only through this link:





Job Type: Full-time



Benefits:

Flexible schedule

Flextime

Paid training

Pay raise

title: Short-Form Video Editor — AI Content Creator, Instagram/TikTok
description:
JOB TITLE: Short-Form Video Editor — AI Content Creator, Instagram/TikTok

SALARY: $1,100 USD/month

TYPE: Full-time (40 hours/week)

LOCATION: Remote (Philippines)

SCHEDULE: Async, with Vancouver overlap (PST) for feedback



---



THE MISSION



Prismiera is a luxury jewelry brand with a short-form video series already producing cinematic content on YouTube — mythology, history, goddess archetypes, and Dark Academia aesthetics.



We need someone to own the Instagram and TikTok side.



This is NOT repurposing YouTube content into different aspect ratios. This is building platform-native content that makes a woman feel powerful in under 20 seconds. You'll create original short-form edits and cinematic skits using AI-generated footage, voiceovers, and atmospheric visuals — designed from the ground up for how Instagram and TikTok audiences actually consume.



The other half of this role: you'll be working with our AI-generated personas who serve as the visual anchor across our short-form content. You'll generate, composite, integrate, and stylize AI character visuals into edits that feel striking and intentional, not gimmicky. If the idea of building a visual identity around an AI persona excites you, keep reading!



---



THE STYLE



The content mix you'll produce:



65% VIBE & SCENARIO — The kind of reel a woman sends to her group chat with no caption because the vibe speaks for itself.



25% CINEMATIC SKITS — High-end, visually stunning executions of viral formats and dark humor. The premise can be slightly unhinged or ironic, but the visuals never break luxury. Think high-end perfume commercial meets TikTok audio trend. The Goddess is never the butt of the joke — she's the punchline, and she always wins.



10% SENSORY / EXPERIMENTAL — Layered, fast, loop-optimized. Visual overload with mythological texture. The algorithmic wildcard.



YES: Aspirational. Lethal elegance. Fashion-edit energy. Dark femininity. Music-driven impact. Content that feels like a luxury car reel meets ancient mythology. Cinematic skits using our AI personas — high-production value, deadpan, surreal, or darkly ironic.



NO: CapCut presets. Generic motivational quote reels. Low-effort vlog-style acting. Slapstick comedy. Anything that looks like it was made in Canva. Anything that cheapens a demi-luxury product.



---



WHAT YOU'LL DO



Create & Adapt: Produce original Instagram/TikTok-native content using AI-generated visuals, trending audio, and our brand's mythology. Some pieces you'll build from scratch. Others you'll adapt from assets in our YouTube production pipeline. Either way, you're not resizing — you're crafting content shaped for how each platform rewards engagement.



Cinematic Skits: Build short-form skits around our AI personas that use trending formats, dark humor, or deadpan irony — executed at a visual level that never drops below luxury. People share it for the joke, follow the page for the aesthetic.



AI Persona Integration: Composite and stylize our AI-generated characters into edits as a visual anchor. She's not a narrator in your content — she's the presence. The face the eye locks onto while mythology and music layers move around her.



AI Content Generation: You won't just receive AI assets — you'll create them. Using tools like Seedance 2.0 and Seedream, you'll generate goddess visuals, atmospheric environments, and character footage that feed directly into your edits. You're both the director and the editor.



Music-Sync Editing: Your cuts, transitions, and visual reveals are married to the beat. You understand rhythm as a storytelling tool. Swells, drops, silence, impact — all intentional.



Platform Instinct: You understand what Instagram rewards vs. what TikTok rewards. You know what gets saved on IG. You know what gets looped on TikTok. You design for those behaviors, not just for visual quality.



Instagram Packaging: Beyond the Reel itself — you contribute to grid cohesion, caption strategy, and Story sequencing. The profile should make someone think "I want to be part of this" within 2 seconds of landing.



Volume at Speed: Once your templates and aesthetic are locked, your turnaround per piece should be significantly faster than building from zero. We expect high output.



---



CORE REQUIREMENTS



After Effects (Strong Plus). Compositing, layering, motion graphics, light/shadow matching. If you have these skills, they'll set you apart. If you don't, strong AI generation skills and visual instinct can compensate.



Premiere Pro. For project handoffs and consistency with the rest of the team.



AI Generation Fluency. This is not optional. You'll be using frontier AI tools daily — not just editing AI output, but generating it. You'll learn to prompt, iterate, select, and refine AI-generated visuals as a core part of your production workflow. We provide paid accounts for all tools (Seedance 2.0, Seedream, and whatever launches next). What we need is someone who treats these tools as creative instruments, not novelties.



The Aesthetic Eye. You instinctively know the difference between aspirational and try-hard. Between elegant and overproduced. Between powerful and loud. Your edits make people feel something before they consciously understand what they're looking at.



Music & Rhythm. You've made edits where the music drives the visuals — AMVs, fashion reels, phonk edits, fan edits, whatever. You understand pacing as a weapon. You know what "the drop" means and you know when NOT to use it.



Loop Thinking. You design edits that reward rewatching. The cut that lands on beat, the detail you only catch the second time, the seamless loop point. You think in circles, not lines.



Strong English. Daily async communication — clear, direct, professional.



Reliable & Coachable. You hit deadlines or warn early. You take direct feedback and iterate fast. You flag problems with options, not excuses.



---



BONUS SKILLS (Not Required, But We'll Notice)



- Color grading — pushing AI output and mixed footage into a consistent luxury palette

- Typography sense — text overlays that feel intentional and premium

- Sound design — SFX layering beyond just music (risers, hits, atmospheric texture)

- Carousel / static design for Instagram companion posts

- Understanding of Instagram analytics — saves, shares, completion rates, and what drives them

- Experience building visual identities for brands or personas on social media



---



WEEKLY OUTPUT



3–5 pieces as you learn the brand aesthetic, lock your editing templates, learn the AI generation tools, and calibrate with the team.



Steady state: 10–15 pieces per week across Instagram and TikTok — a mix of original productions and asset-based adaptations depending on the content calendar.



---



HOW THIS ROLE FITS



Other editors handle cinematic YouTube production and visual effects — you own social media. Same mythology, same brand, different platform behavior. Each editor has a distinct lane, and yours is how Prismiera looks and feels on Instagram and TikTok.



---



SYSTEM REQUIREMENTS



RAM: 16GB minimum (32GB preferred) + SSD

Reliable high-speed internet (upload speed matters)

Must run video editing software and browser-based AI tools simultaneously



---



PAID TRIAL (Shortlisted Candidates)



Before any full-time commitment, we run a weekly paid trial. We provide real assets from our production pipeline, style references, and the AI persona visuals. You deliver content. Trial is compensated at $150 USD at the end of each week. If it's a mutual fit after 30 days, we transition to regular bi-weekly payments (before work).



This protects both sides — you see whether the work fits your strengths, we see whether the output fits our brand.



---



WHAT WE OFFER



- Paid accounts for all frontier AI tools — Seedance 2.0, Seedream, and whatever launches next

- Creative ownership — you're defining how a luxury brand looks and feels on Instagram and TikTok from day one

- An AI persona to build around — not a hypothetical, an active visual identity you'll shape and evolve

- A role that evolves as the tools evolve — this job will look different in six months, and that's the point

- Clear briefs — asset libraries, mood references, and specific direction for every batch

- Direct feedback — specific, constructive notes on every edit

- A brand worth building — Prismiera launches soon. A second AI-native brand is incubating behind it. You're not joining a finished machine. You're helping build one.



---



COMPENSATION



$1,100 USD/month. Paid trial at $150 USD/week, transitioning to bi-weekly payments of $550 (before work) after 30 days.



---



OWNERSHIP & CONFIDENTIALITY



All outputs (edits, project files, prompts, generated assets, persona visuals) are Prismiera property and must remain confidential.



---



HOW TO APPLY



Do not send a generic cover letter.

Use 7 apply points or more when applying.



Subject line: PRISMIERA SOCIAL — [Your Name]



Send the following (in this order):



1. The Vibe Test. Link to ONE edit you've made that is aspirational, aesthetic, or fashion-adjacent — or a skit that demonstrates platform-native virality instinct. Format doesn't matter. The feeling does.



2. The Rhythm Test. Link to ONE edit where the music drives the visuals. Show us your beat-sync instinct. (Can overlap with #1 if one video demonstrates both.)



3. The Creative Instinct Test (3–5 sentences total). You're given a 45-second cinematic video about a queen who poisoned her entire court, a folder of our AI Goddess visuals, and a dark atmospheric beat. Pitch two reworks: (A) a 15-second Instagram Reel designed to get saved — tell us your audio choice, pacing, and what the first 2 seconds look like; and (B) a 15-second cinematic skit that uses dark or deadpan humor without losing the luxury feel.



4. AI Experience (1–2 sentences). What is your experience level with generative AI tools? If none, say so — we'll evaluate the rest on its own merits.



5. Hardware. Your PC specs: CPU / GPU / RAM. Plus your internet upload speed.



6. Turnaround Estimate. Given existing footage, a music track, and an established editing template — how long would a 15–20 second piece take you?



7. The Rewrite. Rewrite this sentence to sound dark, powerful, and aspirational: "She is really cool and strong and she wins."

title: AI Engineer — Agentic AI / Python / API Integration (Full Time)
description:
We're building something most businesses are still only talking about.

Pet Let is an Australian holiday rental company that's been operating since 2009. We manage 61 properties, a team across two countries, and a database of more than 13,000 guests.

We're building a fully AI-powered operation — a network of agents connected to our core business systems that can run guest communications, operations coordination, financial processing, reporting, and marketing autonomously, with a lean human team providing oversight.

We have a diverse tech stack including a property management system, a property operations platform, trust accounting software, and Google Workspace. Your job is to connect all of it to AI — and build agents and live dashboards on top of those connections that actually run parts of this business.

We're at the beginning of this build. We don't have a locked-in stack. We want the right engineer to help us make smart architectural decisions and then execute them fast.



This role is for someone who writes code. Not someone who connects nodes.

If your portfolio is built on n8n, Make, Zapier, or GHL — this is not the role for you. We respect what those tools do. This isn't that.

We need someone who builds from scratch in Python. Who reads API documentation and writes the integration. Who understands agentic AI architecture — giving models structured, permissioned access to real business systems so they can act, not just respond.



What you'll actually be doing

Agent infrastructure is the foundation. You'll build the layer that connects AI models directly to our business platforms — giving them the ability to read booking state, create operations tasks, trigger communications, reconcile financial data, and close the loop without a human in the middle. Whether that's via MCP, function calling, tool use, or whatever approach makes most sense — you'll make that call.

AI agents built on top of that infrastructure — autonomous, logged, observable, and safe. Agents that receive inputs, make decisions, trigger actions, and report back.

Live operational dashboards pulling real data from our systems — giving leadership a single view of the whole business and giving each team exactly what they need to act on. You choose the right tool.

Our goal is to have the first agent live in production within two weeks of starting. That's the pace we're moving at.



Who we're looking for

You're a Python developer who works seriously with AI — whatever model, whatever coding environment, whatever tool-serving architecture you prefer. What matters is that you're genuinely building agentic systems at a deep level, not just connecting existing services through workflow tools.

You think in systems — inputs, context, decision logic, action layer, logging, feedback loop. You've built something real with an AI API in Python and you can show us the code. Not a Loom video of a workflow. Not a Canva deck. Actual code.

You understand what it means to give an AI model the ability to act inside a real system — and why that's fundamentally different from building a chatbot. You know why structured, permissioned tool exposure matters. You've thought about what happens when agents fail in production.

You're the kind of person who locks in with your favourite drink, your favourite snacks, and a hard problem — and surfaces a few hours later with something working. Our CEO is losing sleep because she's so energised by what's possible. We want someone who gets that feeling.

Non-negotiable:



Python — genuinely proficient, evidenced by actual code

Builds with AI tools seriously and daily

Understands agentic AI architecture — tool servers, structured outputs, closed-loop execution

REST API integration built from scratch in code

Has shipped something with an AI API and can show the actual code

Strong written English

Self-directed, fast, and comfortable owning ambiguous problems end to end



Strong advantage:



Experience with MCP, function calling, or equivalent tool-serving architectures

FastAPI or similar Python web frameworks

PostgreSQL or similar database experience

Google Workspace API (Sheets, Chat, Gmail)

React or similar for dashboard frontends

Any background in hospitality, property management, or operational businesses





About the salary — let's be straight with you

We're starting at $800–$1,200 USD/month. We've been burnt before by people who look great on paper,  interview beautifully and can't execute. So we start cautious.

But if you can do what this ad describes — if you can take our vision and actually ship it fast — your value to us goes up dramatically and your rate will reflect that immediately. We'll review your compensation as soon as the first agent is live in production, which could be within two weeks of starting. Regular reviews after that tied directly to delivery.

We're not looking for someone to pay minimally and keep there. We're looking for someone exceptional who gets rewarded for it.



How to apply

We will not consider applications without all three of the following:



Your GitHub profile link — we will look at it before we read anything else

Something you built in Python using an AI API — link to the repo or show us the actual code. Not a workflow video. Not a Canva deck. Code.

Your answer to this question: You need to give an AI model the ability to act inside a property management system — not just read data, but take actions. How do you approach that architecture and what's the first capability you'd expose?



If your answer to question 3 mentions n8n, Make, or Zapier as the solution — please don't apply.  We love your work (we are using those tools now), but thats not what this job is about. 



Pet Let has been making holidays better for pet owners since 2009. We think AI is about to let us do that at a level that simply wasn't possible before. Come help us build it.

title: Virtual Assistant – Daily Ticket Repricing (Web Tools) – 2–3 hrs/day
description:
IMPORTANT: Accuracy is #1. Please read carefully.



I’m hiring a part-time virtual assistant to do daily pricing updates for a large set of listings (~200/day) using a web-based ticket inventory/pricing tool and a separate source website for availability/pricing checks.



You will follow a strict checklist and adjust listing prices to stay competitive while meeting required profit minimums.



Daily Responsibilities



Log into my pricing tool and perform a daily “sync/catch-up” action on inventory.



Review each listing and verify the event’s availability and current price on the source website.



If the event is not available (sold out/waitlist/unavailable), you will pause/deactivate the listing and notify me.



If you cannot locate the event or you are unsure it matches, you must not change anything and notify me.



Update prices to stay competitive (undercut by a small amount when appropriate) only when you can still meet minimum profit requirements shown in the tool.



Rules (must follow exactly)



Most listings are GA or VIP.



You must use the tool’s “Proceeds/Net” section to confirm minimum profit:



GA: minimum $12



VIP: minimum $25



If being cheaper would drop below minimum profit, do not undercut.



When comparing competitor prices, ignore competitor listings with quantity = 1.



You are not allowed to add new events, edit quantities, or modify listing details beyond price.



If any of my listings show quantity under 3, notify me.



Preferred setup (not required, but a big plus)



Desktop/laptop with a large screen or dual monitors / extended display so you can view two sites at once.



Schedule



2–3 hours/day, 7 days/week (14–21 hrs/week). Preferred window: 7–10 AM ET (flexible ±1 hour).



Confidentiality



You will have access to private business workflows and systems. You must keep all information confidential and not share screenshots, data, logins, or processes.



Trial + Growth



Paid test + 3-day trial.



Long-term role for high performers, with opportunities to take on additional admin tasks over time (spreadsheets + payout reconciliation).



To Apply (required)



Are you available 7 days/week for 2–3 hours/day consistently?



What device setup do you have (single screen, large monitor, or dual monitor)?



If you can’t confidently match the event between the pricing tool and the source website, what will you do?



If being $1 cheaper would drop below the minimum profit requirement, what will you do?



Include the word ORANGE in the first sentence of your application so I know you read the instructions.

title: Administration Assistant - Mortgage Brokering (Australian Firm)

Why work with Talentport?

We are an established Australian trade services group handling projects across construction, civil works and specialist maintenance, finance and marketing with a diverse array of clients.



Overview:

We are seeking a highly organised, proactive, and detail-oriented Virtual Assistant

to support our client and their growing mortgage brokerage business. The ideal candidate will possess

strong communication skills, hands-on experience with *Connective Mercury*, and the

ability to manage administrative tasks with precision and discretion as an essential requirement.



You will play a vital role in supporting brokers, assisting clients, and ensuring their loan processes

run efficiently from start to finish.



Key Responsibilities

1. Administrative Support (Critical Organisational Skills Required)

- Manage and respond to
s, calls, and messages from clients, lenders, and other stakeholders.

- Schedule meetings, client consultations, and follow-ups using calendar tools.

- Maintain organised digital filing systems for client documents and internal records.

Assist in gathering and organising client documentation (e.g., income

verification, credit reports, bank statements). Provide regular updates to clients regarding their loan status. Help clients understand the next steps in the mortgage process and address general inquiries.



3. Document Management

Lodge supporting documents and manage correspondence via Mercury.



Required Skills and; Qualifications

- Experience using Connective Mercury CRM is preferred but not essential.

- Previous experience in mortgage broking, lending, or real estate preferred.

- Excellent verbal and written communication skills.

- High level of accuracy, discretion, and professionalism.

- Strong organisational and multitasking abilities.

- Proficient in tools such as Google Workspace, Microsoft Office, Zoom, and cloud file management (e.g., Dropbox, Google Drive).

Ability to work independently with minimal supervision.

- Understanding of Australian mortgage products and compliance processes is a strong advantage.



Work Schedule:

- Fully remote

- Flexible working hours

- 7am-3pm or 8am-4pm

- Full Time



A portfolio of showing your organisational skills will be highly regarded

title: Shopify Product Listing Virtual Assistant
description:
We are developing a home decor store optimized for Google Shopping and actively testing a large number of products.



Your primary responsibility will be to upload products efficiently, accurately, and in a professional manner using our structured workflow.



This is not a branding or marketing position.

This is a role focused on execution.



If you are highly detail-focused and able to work quickly, you will thrive in this position.



Responsibilities



• Upload 50 products per 2/hr shift

• Create clean product pages in Shopify

• Use Nano Banana (AI image tool) to create 4–5 improved product images

• Rewrite product titles and descriptions (short, clean, not hype)

• Remove supplier branding or watermarks

• Follow a consistent product page structure



What You Will NOT Do



• No use of false urgency or misleading scarcity tactics

• No creation or use of fake reviews

• No unprofessional or deceptive visual edits

• No unsupported medical or health claims

• No low-quality or irrelevant product images



Our focus is clean, professional, and straightforward execution.



Product Image Rules



Each product must include:



• Clean product image

• Close-up detail

• Lifestyle image

• Benefit image (minimal text overlay)



All images must look:

• Branded

• High quality

• Not like AliExpress



You will use Nano Banana to improve competitor images and create better versions.



Product Page Structure



Each product page will include:



• Clean title (Brand + Keywords)

• Short intro paragraph

• 1–3 standard trust sections (same on all products)

• Related collection



Pay



$4–6/hour depending on experience.



If performance is strong, long term position available.



Requirements



• Experience with Shopify product listing uploads

• Experience with Kopy (importing app) A MUST

• Basic image editing knowledge

• Can follow instructions exactly

• Fast and detail-oriented

• Good English
